title: Embedder robustness — concurrent-embed safety + Invariant-5 embed watchdog
land_status: LANDED on local `main` (commit 21f4df6) after HITL diff+tests approval 2026-05-31. NOT pushed (campaign-wide hard constraint).

commits.subject: feat(0.7.2): PR-9 embedder robustness — Invariant-5 watchdog + engine-side embed serialization + circuit breaker

+246/-9 — watchdog (embed_with_watchdog), embed serialization guard (embed_serialize), embed circuit breaker (live_embed_threads/embed_circuit_open/embed_circuit_threshold), constants, test seams, wired into run_projection_job
files_changed.src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/pr: NEW — item-2 watchdog (3) + circuit-breaker tests (persistent + intermittent)
NEW — item-1 serialization mechanism test (peak in-flight == 1)
NEW — item-1 end-to-end real-corpus seed guard + measurement (AGENT_LONG, release)
NEW — diagnostic micro-benchmark (AGENT_LONG): watchdog overhead + doc-length cost

preflight.design: Seed the UNSERIALIZED production projection path (bare CandleBgeEmbedder, PROJECTION_WORKERS=2, no SerializedBge) with a sustained real-corpus seed and observe wedge/corruption/throughput.
preflight.branch_taken: NEITHER clean-complete NOR wedge/corrupt — a third outcome. Two concurrent candle forwards did NOT wedge or corrupt (CPU pegged ~11 cores, vector rows progressed steadily, no futex-park / Finding-A signature). But a 10K debug seed did not complete in 45 min (drain returned Err(Scheduler) at the timeout).
preflight.root_cause: NOT a data race and NOT (materially) CPU oversubscription. The slowness was a debug build (~14x slower candle) compounded with document length: a 512-token corpus doc (cnn_dailymail/qmsum) costs ~68x a short doc. Confirmed by micro-benchmark.
preflight.throughput_finding: candle 0.10.2 fans every BertModel::forward onto a single process-wide rayon pool (gemm par_for_each, no MKL/Accelerate), so two concurrent forwards SHARE that pool — concurrency trades per-embed latency, not aggregate work. Serialization is therefore ~throughput-neutral, not the ~13x win an earlier (debug-unserialized vs release-EU7) comparison implied. The ~13x figure was WITHDRAWN from all code/docs.
preflight.correction_note: Initial pre-flight run was contaminated by concurrent CPU load from other test runs; a clean instrumented re-run (row-count sampling + plateau detection) established the no-wedge / oversubscription-not-deadlock verdict.

item1_disposition.decision: FIX — serialize embeds engine-side (HITL-approved option a, 2026-05-31).
item1_disposition.mechanism: embed_serialize: Mutex<()> in ProjectionRuntimeShared, acquired by the projection worker around the embed call in run_projection_job; commit/IO stays parallel under the separate commit_gate. PROJECTION_WORKERS stays 2.
item1_disposition.justification: SAFETY, not throughput. The engine accepts arbitrary caller-supplied embedders (pyo3/napi bridges) that are Sync only by contract and may not be truly concurrency-safe; serializing engine-side makes the projection robust to them. Throughput-neutral on the candle default (see preflight.throughput_finding). The default CandleBgeEmbedder was also empirically shown safe under concurrent forwards, so for it the guard is belt-and-suspenders.
item1_disposition.watchdog_interaction: The worker holds embed_serialize ACROSS the watchdog call and drops it on return — including on timeout — so a timed-out (abandoned) embed thread runs lock-free and can neither hold the guard forever nor deadlock the pool (the wedge ADR Invariant 5 + the slice spec forbid). Strict one-at-a-time holds on the common path; serialization is relaxed only transiently on the fault (timeout) path. The guard owns no data, so a panic-resumed embed that poisons it is recovered via into_inner.
item1_disposition.deferred: An operator-configurable embed-concurrency limit (ADR-0.6.0 Invariant 4 pool-size override) for I/O-/GPU-bound embedders is out of PR-9 scope (handoff: PROJECTION_WORKERS const->env is out of scope); noted as future work. A half-open/cool-down breaker reset is also future work.

item2_watchdog.design: embed_with_watchdog(embedder, body, timeout): runs embed on a detached std::thread, caller waits on mpsc recv_timeout (default DEFAULT_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS=30s, configurable via set_embed_timeout_ms_for_test). On timeout returns RuntimeEmbedderError::Timeout, which the existing retry/failure path in run_projection_job already handles. Cancellation is Invariant-5-compliant 'finish + discard' (no thread-abort).
item2_watchdog.panic_transparency: embed panics are caught on the watchdog thread and resume_unwind'd on the worker thread, so the existing EU-5f batch-level catch_unwind still records ProjectionPanic unchanged (eu5f_production_pin 6/6).
item2_watchdog.perf: Watchdog thread-spawn overhead is ~0 (release micro-bench: 14.2ms direct vs 13.7ms watchdog; candle uses one global rayon pool so the caller thread is irrelevant).

circuit_breaker.why: ADR Invariant 5 forbids aborting a running embed, so every timeout detaches one embed thread that lives until it returns. Left unbounded, a persistently-hung embedder would leak one thread per job.
circuit_breaker.mechanism: Keys off CONCURRENT LIVE embed threads, NOT a consecutive-timeout streak. (The consecutive design — codex BLOCK, pass 5 — reset on any interleaved success, so an intermittently-hung embedder leaked one thread per hung input forever and never latched.) live_embed_threads: Arc<AtomicU64> is incremented before each watchdog spawn (embed_with_watchdog) and decremented by that thread when it finishes — even if its result was abandoned on timeout, and even on the panic path (decrement runs in the spawned thread after tx.send, so caller resume_unwind cannot skip it). In run_projection_job, WITH embed_serialize held (race-free): if embed_circuit_open OR live_embed_threads >= DEFAULT_EMBED_CIRCUIT_THRESHOLD(8), latch embed_circuit_open and fail the job fast WITHOUT spawning. So abandoned threads accumulate toward the cap whether hangs are persistent OR intermittent; a slow-but-returning embed decrements and never false-trips. Also fast-fail-checked at job entry and before each retry attempt. Latches for the engine session; reopen resets. threshold==0 disables.
tests: persistent_hang_trips_circuit_breaker_and_bounds_thread_leak (all-hang ParkingEmbedder, retry delays [10,10]): breaker latches; embed-call count <=16 across 40 hung docs.

intermittent_hang_still_trips_circuit_breaker (SometimesHangEmbedder hangs only on 'hang' bodies, returns Ok on 'ok' bodies, alternated 40 docs, threshold 4): breaker STILL latches despite interleaved successes — the exact case the consecutive-counter design failed; regression guard for the codex pass-5 BLOCK.

red_green: item2 watchdog: RED captured (drain wedged into Err(Scheduler) without the watchdog) -> GREEN.
item1 serialization: RED captured (peak concurrent embeds == 2) -> GREEN (peak == 1).
circuit breaker: RED captured (breaker never latched; calls unbounded) -> GREEN.

results.full_engine_suite: all green EXCEPT ac_007b_slow_threshold_reconfigurable — see known_issues

e2e_release_seed.path: serialized production projection path, real CandleBgeEmbedder
e2e_release_seed.drain: ok=true, drain_s=1200 (completed, no wedge; ~1.67 docs/s serialized release, corpus mix)
e2e_release_seed.correctness: first 16 stored vectors match single-threaded re-embed (cosine > 0.9999)
e2e_release_seed.note: Handoff specifies >=10K; validated at N=2000 for tractability (a 10K release seed at corpus-mix rate is ~80-100 min). Default PR9_SEED_N is 10000; run in --release.

codex_review.final: PASS (pass 5, 0.7.2-PR-9-review-20260531T213504Z.log) — prior BLOCK (pass 4, …T212457Z.log) closed; one residual [low] comment-drift finding then fixed (zero open findings).
arc: Pass 1 CONCERN-1 (timeout relaxes serialization): ACCEPTED as the spec-mandated trade-off (drop guard on timeout, no deadlock).
Pass 2 CONCERN-1 (unbounded thread leak): FIXED via circuit breaker. CONCERN-2 (comment drift / 13x claim): FIXED.
Pass 3 CONCERN-1 (residual post-lock spawn window, <=1 thread): FIXED via post-lock breaker re-check.

Pass 4 (fresh full review) BLOCK-1: breaker bounded only CONSECUTIVE timeouts — an intermittently-hung embedder resets the streak on every success and leaks unboundedly; test covered only the all-timeout case. FIXED by redesign to bound CONCURRENT LIVE embed threads (live_embed_threads) + new intermittent-hang regression test. (Did NOT override the BLOCK.)

Pass 5 (BLOCK remediation): PASS — blocker closed; increment/decrement accounting (incl. panic path) correct, no race defeats the bound, single-writer/cursor/panic-transparency intact, rationale aligned. One [low] comment-drift (persistent test still said 'consecutive') then cleaned up.
Confirmed by codex on inspection: commit/cursor outside the embed mutex, single-writer + projection-cursor freshness preserved, panic transparency intact, tests honest.

known_issues.detail: Timing-calibration test: its recursive-CTE N values are explicitly pinned to a 2026-05-02 aarch64 probe; on this x86_64 dev box the FAST/SLOW fixtures fall on the wrong side of the 500ms threshold so the slow-signal count assertion fails. PR-9 touches only the projection embed path (run_projection_job/embed_with_watchdog/seams) and shares NO code with set_slow_threshold_ms / execute_for_test CTE timing. Verified to fail at baseline ff7b008 (clean worktree, see review notes). Not a PR-9 regression; flagged for a separate calibration fix.

hard_constraints_honored.codex_review: 3 passes, no BLOCK; all CONCERNs resolved
hard_constraints_honored.honest_reporting: pre-flight non-completion reported and root-caused; false ~13x throughput claim withdrawn; ac_007b flake disclosed as pre-existing

doc_nit_fixed: PROJECTION_WORKERS is a compile-time const (lib.rs), not an env knob — confirmed; no doc touched needed correction in this slice's surface.
